label: The mirror
sub: what I see when it’s just me
framing: Where I argue with myself. The quadrant I write in when I don’t know what I believe yet, where first drafts go to be held up to the light.

statement.text: prototype rapidly with AI & sticky notes

items.title: Designing AI products
items.dek: Humanity, in interfaces, is not about imitation.

items.title: How I create rapid prototypes
items.dek: Not despite AI. Because of it.

label: The practice
framing: What I kick off when nobody asked. Meetups, experiments, rituals I run on myself, the stuff that wouldn’t exist if I waited for permission.

items.title: Select-and-fill prompts for chatbots
items.dek: I don't need to type the full sentence

items.title: Voice & gesture — anything but text-based prompt
items.dek: I am too lazy to type

items.title: Doing nothing but just watching AI-AI interaction
Leave a message to your future self — AI delivers it at the right moment
items.dek: There is no boundary of time

items.title: The chain of thought for users
items.dek: Thinking out loud had a shape

items.title: AI can pushes back, complete, or advocates for you
items.dek: AI had a point of view

label: Paying attention
sub: paying attention to what I notice in other people
framing: Watching, listening, not-yet-acting. Most of what I’ve learned about design I’ve learned by watching people describe their work out loud.

label: The work
framing: The finished, shipped, collaborative stuff. Where thinking has to become a decision and other people have to live with it.

items.title: Shopping doesn’t have to start with a search
